{{nihongo|'''Ochi no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|小市国造|''ochi no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''ochi kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled the eastern portion of [[Iyo Province]] under the later {{ill|ritsuryō provinces|ja|令制国}}, corresponding to the eastern part of modern [[Ehime Prefecture]].

== Overview ==
According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Ōjin]], Koji-no-Mikoto, grandson of Ōniikawa-no-Mikoto who shared the same ancestor as the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|lt=Mononobe-no-Muraji}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko. Additionally, in the Mononobe genealogy of the "Tenson Hongi", Mononobe-no-Ōochi-no-Muraji, son of Ōniikawa-no-Mikoto, is given as the ancestor of the Ochi-no-Atai.

The [[descendants]] of Koji-no-Mikoto settled widely in [[Ochi District]] and became the {{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (with the [[kabane]] of {{ill|Atai|ja|直 (カバネ)}}). The Ochi clan were appointed kuni no miyatsuko, {{ill|kōrizō|ja|評造}}, kōri-no-kami, and {{ill|district magistrate|ja|郡司|lt=gunji}} generation after generation, and a member of the clan, {{ill|Ochi no Tamazumi|ja|越智玉澄}}, is said to have founded {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|lt=Ōmishima Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt_ja=大三島大山祇神社}}.

== References ==
* Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Hensan Iinkai (ed.), ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten (Ehime-ken)'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1991, ISBN 4-04-001380-8, pp. 26, 182.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おちのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:伊予国]]
[[Category:伊予国の氏族]]
[[Category:物部氏|-おちのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:愛媛県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:小市国造]]
